description: Batons of potato that are fried until crisp outside and tender inside. They are commonly salted and served as a side dish or snack, with many regional cuts and sauces.
preparation_steps: Cut potatoes into even sticks or wedges.
Rinse and dry well to remove excess surface starch.
Fry once at a lower temperature to cook the interior, or bake/air-fry until tender if using an alternative method.
Fry again at a higher temperature until golden and crisp if deep-frying.
Drain and season immediately, then serve hot.

health_advice.benefits: Provides carbohydrates for energy and can be satisfying in small portions.
health_advice.risks: Deep-fried versions can be high in calories, fat, and sodium, especially with heavy sauces or cheese toppings.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate portions and choose baked or air-fried versions when possible.

dietary_notes.note: Usually vegetarian and vegan. Gluten-free suitability depends on fryer cross-contact and added seasonings or coatings.

allergens: Possible gluten from shared fryers or coatings
Milk if topped with cheese or creamy sauces
